MODULE Two – Business Launch Formula – Part 2

Executive Summary:
Key Takeaways:
• 	There are two main types of lists:
o 	A prospect list
o 	A buyer (owner) list
• 	A person on your buyers list is worth 15-20X a person on your prospect list. Once you
sell something to a customer once, it’s a lot easier the second time. Some people do
launches to their list once a month
• 	To prevent your prospects from being overwhelmed or fatigued by launch emails,
you’ll encourage them to opt in to the specific launch list. You should offer some form
of great prelaunch content when asking them to opt in
• 	Every business is different and how you organize your list will be different. A lead
(new person on your list) is most responsive when they first opt in to your list - over
time they become less responsive
• 	When you do a launch and ask people to opt in again it usually reinvigorates the list
Example: John Gallagher
• 	When John Gallagher started he had just a newsletter list, which was a prospect list.
Once they bought his board game they moved to the buyers (Owners) list
• 	Next let’s assume he was going to do a launch for his herbal kit. He would then send
an email to inform his list about the herbal kit product opportunity and ask them to
opt in - this would be sent to both the prospects and the buyers of the board game
• 	If they purchase the herbal kit they would move to a new buyers list - so you’ll have
multiple buyers lists. Now he wants to sell his membership site - again the process is
repeated. An opportunity to opt in to his launch list is sent to the prospects and
buyers of the first two products
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

• 	When John first started out he did a full out launch to his entire list. but in general
you want to create a new launch list with each new product. This is especially true if
it’s different from your first product, or different from the main reason they signed
up for your list
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT:
In the last video I walked through some of the results that John has had in his
business. Let’s look at how you do this, how he did it, how he managed it, and how
you’re going to do it as well.
Let’s just say this is your list. One of the fundamental things that you have to
understand is that you will end up with a bunch of different lists. There are a couple
primary types of lists. There’s the prospect list, the people who haven’t bought from
you yet. Then there’s the buyers list, and that’s the people that have bought from you.
In our business we call it an owners list.
This is the prospect list. I’m going to write it really small. When John started
out he had those 1800 people on his prospect list.
This is a newsletter. Every month he published a newsletter. This will go
basically forever until someone unsubscribes. This is your main list, for John it was
his newsletter list. What he did was within this list he ran a little launch sequence for
the game. Maybe it’s launch day for the game, and now if someone buys from him,
then they join his owners or buyers list. Basically when someone buys from you they
go on your buyers list. Let’s say this is the launch of his board game and now these
people went into his buyers list.
Now, his next launch is for the herbal kit, so he would there is—this is what
we call a launch list. He would basically create a squeeze page. He would send people
to the squeeze page. He might send out an email that says, “If you’re interested in
being on the list for the herbal kit.”
That’s typically not what you’ll say, but you’ll talk about whatever the
opportunity is for the herbal kit, send people to a squeeze page and now he’s got
them on his launch list. In the launch list, maybe there’s two, three or four emails,
then there’s a chance for them to buy right here.
This is where they bought for the board game. This is where they buy for the
herbal kit. Now what do you do? If they buy the herbal kit they go on to the buyers
list for the herbal kit. Now you’ve got a couple of different buyers lists.
Depending on what they’re buying and how your business lays there might be
one buyers list. All the different list systems manage lists and prospects in different
ways, but conceptually you’ve now got buyers.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

I’m sorry I missed another key piece… Now you’ve got emails going out to the
buyers list, follow up emails. Then somewhere in there you might tell them about the
herbal kit. They’re coming into the launch list. This launch list is getting fed buy your
prospect list and by your buyers list. You’re probably following up with these people
on this buyers list. The follow up sequence for the buyers list is a really high value
thing to create.
Now he’s coming up on his membership site, and he creates another launch
list now for his membership site. He’s going to have a whole sequence there. Now
what he’s going to do is he’s going to peel people off. That’s what I call it. You peel
people off. In other words, you ask people if they’re interested, you send them to a
squeeze page and now they’re joining your launch list.
He’s got people joining his launch list for his membership site. You’re going to
mail this buyers list and send them in. You’re going to mail this buyers list and send
them in.
One of the things about buyer’s lists is, in my experience someone on your
buyers list is worth 15 to 20 times what someone on your prospect list is worth. I
would do a launch, back in my stock market days I’d have 5,000 people on this
prospect list. Then I’d have 500 people on my buyers list in the early days.
I would come up with a new product and do a promotion, and I would do
more sales to this 500-person list than that 5,000-person list. Once you sell
something to someone the first time, it’s much easier to sell something to them the
second time.
John takes them through the sequence here for the membership site, send
through a sequence. Now they get a chance to buy right here. It ends up with a
decision to buy. If they buy, what happens? They go on his buyers list. Basically
they’re part of the membership site which is a buyers list.
The next time he might want to do a home study course and he creates
another launch list for that with a sequence for that. What’s he going to do? He’s
going to peel people off his main list, in other words give them the opportunity to join
this list. He’ll have a squeeze page. The squeeze page will offer some great Prelaunch
Content.
They come from his prospect list. Each one of these buyers lists he’ll also mail
each one of them and send them in here. He’ll create another launch list.
What you’re doing here is—if you’re going to do another launch for this, an
all-out launch and a whole bunch of emails, maybe these people are used to getting

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – Business Launch Formula – Part 2
6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
emails a couple of times a month and you’re going to keep up that publication
schedule. You don’t want to start hammering them with all these emails for your
upcoming launch.
Generally, you’ll create that launch list, then you’ll encourage people to move
over from this list to this list. When I say encourage them that means you’re going to
mail them and send them to a squeeze page. The squeeze offers some cool things,
some cool videos, bonuses, whatever.
That leads them into the sequence.
Generally, with these launch lists, they’re sort of an episodic type thing. When
someone goes on my launch list, I’m emailing them through the launch sequence,
after that launch sequence is over I’m usually not mailing that list again because
they’re still over on this list.
The other thing is if you move them over to this launch list here and you send
a whole bunch of emails, and usually if you’re going to launch you do send a bunch of
emails. Maybe at some point they decide that they’re not interested. They can
unsubscribe but they’ll only be unsubscribing from this list.
If they want to unsubscribe from all of your lists they can do that as well, but
generally they’ll unsubscribe from this list but they’ll still be on your main list or on
your buyers list. This is how you can be, basically, doing a launch.
In most of my businesses I’ll do them three or four times a year. I know
people that are doing a lunch every single month. How do you do a launch to your
prospect list every month? If each one of these dots were a month, now all of a
sudden you send them a whole bunch of email here and a whole bunch of email here
for each different launch you might have some list fatigue.
Instead what you do is you give them the opportunity to join these launch
lists and take part in that launch and get the great Prelaunch Content that you’re
going to put out. This gives you the opportunity to continue to launch after launch
after launch, not burn out your buyers list and just keep on rolling along. Then when
you come up with another idea for another product you just create another launch
list and another sequence and you’re sending them over.
If you’re just start starting out—when John did his first launch he didn’t come
out with a separate launch list. He just did his full out launch to his full list. In
general, you typically want to do a new launch list when you’re coming out with a
new product, especially if it’s somewhat of a departure from what you’ve done in the
past.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

They bought the board game. They’re getting a newsletter, but that board
game might be really different from the home study course. The home study course
might be on flower essences or something which is related and a great fit for his list,
but slightly different. Maybe not everyone here is interested in flower essences, so
you put them over on that list for people that are interested in that flower essence
launch.
That is how you do it. That’s how you orchestrate this without burning out
your list and this is how you continue to build that list relationship. Every business
will be different. How you organize your lists will be different. It will evolve over
time.
One final piece is, when you get people to go through this opt-in process, an
opt-in for
that new list, that reinvigorates the lead. The lead being the person on the list.
In other words, when someone first joins your list, they are most responsive when
they first join your list. Then over time on average they get less responsive. They see a
bunch of your emails and get less responsive over time.
That’s just a rule of lists that goes back 100 years. When you get them to step
up and opt in again, that rewarms them up and that really reinvigorates the list. It is
generally a great thing when you’re going into a launch for a new product to create
that new launch list.
That’s sort of the organization of lists within the Business Launch Formula.
Again, there are lots of different ways you can do this, but conceptually this is how it
goes.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
